verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a sperm surface protein that is exposed on the outer leaflet of the sperm plasma membrane via a GPI anchor, but only during or after the acrosome reaction — a sperm-specific, cell-state-dependent exocytotic event triggered during fertilization. Before acrosome reaction, IZUMO1 is sequestered within the acrosome, an exocytotic vesicle. Upon acrosomal exocytosis, IZUMO1-containing membranes fuse with the plasma membrane, stably exposing IZUMO1 on the sperm surface where it mediates binding to the egg zona pellucida receptor JUNO. This is a classic example of induced surface exposure via exocytosis of a specialized organellar compartment in a germ cell.
